title: dalbergia assamica
intro: Dalbergia assamica, also known as Indian rosewood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to the Indian subcontinent. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Dalbergia assamica include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against infections, among others. Traditional herbal preparations of the plant include oil, powder, and decoction, which are often used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat a range of health issues.
